characters.name: Male saint (possibly Saint Anthony or a Franciscan)
Female saint (possibly Saint Clare or a Dominican)

symbols: book held by the female saint — devotion to Scripture and the teaching office
dark and white habits — the distinct religious orders each saint represents
narrow panel format — altarpiece wings framing a central devotional image
description: Two narrow gilded panels side by side, each containing a standing haloed figure. The left panel shows a bearded man in a dark blue-brown hooded robe, with a golden halo, standing against a pink background. The right panel shows a figure in a white habit and veil, holding a dark book, also haloed, standing against a similar background. Both figures are rendered in the early Renaissance style with simple, dignified poses. The gold-leafed frames show age and wear.
